title: Figure 5 in In vitro and in vivo antileishmanial activity of B-acetyl-digitoxin, a cardenolide of Digitalis lanata potentially useful to treat visceral leishmaniasis
author: Freitas, Camila S.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 5. Splenic parasitism estimated by quantitative PCR. Leishmania infantum-infected mice (n = 6 per group) were submitted to the distinct treatment regimens and, 15 days post-therapy, they were euthanized and their spleens were collected to evaluate parasitism by qPCR. Results were expressed as the number of parasites per total DNA, and bars represent the mean ± standard deviation of the groups. (*) indicates a significant difference as compared to the saline and B/Mic groups (p <0.05). (**) indicates a statistically significant difference as compared to the miltefosine group (p <0.05). (***) indicates a statistically significant difference as compared to the b-AD group (p <0.05).
